sections.heading: Overview
sections.body:
In this guide, you can learn how to connect to MongoDB instances by using
a SOCKS5 proxy. SOCKS5 is a standardized protocol for connecting
to network services through a proxy server.

> **Tip:** To learn more about the SOCKS5 protocol, see the Wikipedia entry on
> [SOCKS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/w/index.php?title=SOCKS\&oldid=1160087146).

sections.heading: Install the socks Package
sections.body:
Starting in version 6.0 of the Node.js driver, you must install
the `socks` package to use SOCKS5 proxy support in your
application. You can install `socks` by running the following command
in your shell:

```bash
npm i socks
```

sections.heading: SOCKS5 Client Options
sections.body:
You can set options in your `MongoClientOptions` instance or
in your connection URI to configure SOCKS5 proxy support for
your connection. The following table describes the client options
related to SOCKS5:

| Name          | Accepted Values      | Default Value | Description                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------- | -------------------- | ------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| proxyHost     | string               | null          | Specifies the SOCKS5 proxy IPv4 address, IPv6 address, or domain name.                                                                       |
| proxyPort     | non-negative integer | null          | Specifies the TCP port number of the SOCKS5 proxy server. If you set the proxyHost option, the value of this option defaults to 1080.        |
| proxyUsername | string               | null          | Specifies the username for authentication to the SOCKS5 proxy server. If you set this option to a zero-length string, the driver ignores it. |
| proxyPassword | string               | null          | Specifies the password for authentication to the SOCKS5 proxy server. If you set this option to a zero-length string, the driver ignores it. |

> **Important:** The driver throws an error if you set the `proxyPort`,
> `proxyUsername`, or `proxyPassword` options without setting the
> `proxyHost` option.

sections.heading: Example
Additional Information
sections.body:
For more information about SOCKS5 proxy support, see the
[MongoDB SOCKS5 specification](https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/socks5-support/socks5.rst).

sections.heading: API Documentation
sections.body:
To learn more about the methods and types discussed in this
guide, see the following API Documentation:

- [MongoClientOptions](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/interfaces/MongoClientOptions.html)

- [MongoClient](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/classes/MongoClient.html)

- [ProxyOptions](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1/interfaces/ProxyOptions.html)
